2605.31250.title: Entropic Projection Alignment: Estimating, Explaining, and Improving Model Performance Under Distribution Shift
2605.31250.paper_summary: We propose a unified framework for addressing three key challenges of distribution shift: (1) estimating a model's performance on an unlabeled target domain, (2) explaining the shift by identifying the features responsible, and (3) improving the target domain performance. Our method, Entropic Projection Alignment (EPA), aligns the source distribution to the target by matching carefully selected moments while simultaneously minimising the KL divergence from the source. This formulation yields a unique closed-form solution for importance weights, achieving robustness through implicit variance control. Drawing on domain adaptation theory, we establish that moment matching is sufficient for reliable estimation and adaptation, avoiding the need for full density ratio recovery. Extensive experiments, together with strong theoretical guarantees, demonstrate that EPA consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines while offering substantial computational efficiency.
2605.31250.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一個統一框架，以解決分佈轉移的三個關鍵挑戰：<lb/>（1）估計模型在未標記目標領域上的性能，<lb/>（2）通過識別負責的特徵來解釋轉移，<lb/>（3）改善目標領域的性能。<lb/>我們的方法，熵投影對齊（EPA），通過匹配精心選擇的矩來將源分佈對齊到目標，同時最小化來自源的KL散度。<lb/>這一公式為重要性權重提供了一個獨特的封閉形式解，通過隱式方差控制實現了穩健性。<lb/>基於領域適應理論，我們建立了矩匹配對於可靠估計和適應是足夠的，避免了對全密度比恢復的需求。<lb/>大量實驗以及強有力的理論保證表明，EPA在計算效率上提供了顯著的優勢，並且始終超越了最先進的基準。
2605.31250.author: Salim I. Amoukou et.al.
2605.31250.authors: Salim I. Amoukou, Emanuele Albini, Tom Bewley, Saumitra Mishra, Manuela Veloso
